content:
✨ WifiForge | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Training cybersecurity professionals and students on Wi-Fi attack vectors and wireless capabilities in a simulated environment.
✨ Key Features: • Safe and legal Wi-Fi hacking training environment • Automated network and tool setup for labs • Based on open-source mininet-wifi • Removes need for physical hardware for attacks • Supports various Wi-Fi exploitation labs
📖 Summary: WifiForge is a Python-based tool designed to provide a safe and legal environment for learning Wi-Fi hacking techniques. It automates the setup of networks and tools required for various Wi-Fi exploitation labs, leveraging the open-source mininet-wifi framework.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 dockerscan | Go
🎯 Primary Use Case: Scanning Docker containers and images for security vulnerabilities, compliance issues, and hidden threats.
✨ Key Features: • CIS Docker Benchmark v1.7.0 compliance checking • Supply Chain Attack Detection (Imageless Container, Cryptocurrency Miner, Backdoored Library, Image Signature Verification, Phishing Content, Malicious Network Destinations) • Advanced Secrets Detection (Cloud Providers, Version Control, AI/ML APIs) • Written in Go for speed and concurrency
📖 Summary: DockerScan v2.0 is a comprehensive security scanner for Docker containers and images, rewritten in Go. It integrates multiple scanning techniques, including CIS benchmarks, supply chain attack detection, and advanced secrets detection, to ensure container security and compliance.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 tuxmate | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: Simplifying and standardizing the process of installing applications across different Linux distributions by generating safe, inspectable installation commands.
✨ Key Features: • Cross-distro generation (apt, pacman, dnf, zypper, nix, Flatpak, Snap, Homebrew) • Native-first resolution with fallbacks • Safe generation with inspectable output and package checks • Parallel Flatpak installs • Homebrew-aware output
📖 Summary: TuxMate is a cross-distro command generator for installing applications on Linux. It allows users to select applications once and generate inspectable installation commands or scripts tailored for various Linux distributions and package managers, ensuring safe and understandable execution.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

💡 dir2txt | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Consolidating entire project codebases into a single text file for analysis by Large Language Models (LLMs), code reviews, or archival purposes.
✨ Key Features: • Recursively scans all files in a directory • Skips binary files • Supports .dir2txtignore for custom ignore patterns • Automatically removes code comments • Estimates token count and checks against model limits
📖 Summary: dir2txt is a Python script that recursively scans a project directory, extracts the content of all non-binary files, and consolidates it into a single text file. It offers features like ignoring specific files, removing code comments, and estimating token counts, making it useful for feeding codebases to LLMs, code reviews, or creating project backups.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 Constrict | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Compressing video files to meet specific size limits for uploads or storage, without relying on online services.
✨ Key Features: • Compress videos to target file sizes • Automatic calculation of ABR, resolution, framerate, and audio quality • Bulk compression • Customizable framerate limits • Choice of codecs (H.264, HEVC, AV1, VP9)
📖 Summary: Constrict is a desktop application designed to compress video files to a specific target file size. It automates the process of determining optimal encoding parameters like bitrate, resolution, and framerate, offering a user-friendly interface for bulk video compression locally.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source
